name: Millhaven Town Hall
description: A two-story stone building on the north side of the market square, the most official-looking structure in Millhaven. The ground floor is the public hall where the council meets, disputes are arbitrated, and proclamations are posted on the notice board outside. The upper floor houses the town records, the tax ledger, and Lestelle Brightbinder's office — identifiable by the color-coded filing system visible through the window. The building was a merchant's warehouse before the council bought it thirty years ago, and it still feels more practical than grand. The front steps are where people sit to gossip, eat lunch, or wait for someone official to notice them.

sounds: Lestelle's quill scratching — she writes constantly
The thud of binder covers being opened and closed
Arguments in the council chamber carrying through the walls
Aldovar's measured, gravelly voice calling for order
Sylvain's smooth voice making a point no one asked for
Boots on stone floors, echoing in the stairwell
smells: Aldovar's pipe tobacco — he only smokes when stressed, so frequently
Bread from the Green Gate Bakery — someone always brings lunch
atmosphere.lighting: Large windows on both floors. Well-lit during the day. Candelabras for evening council sessions. Lestelle's office has the best window in the building.

layout.name: The Council Chamber
layout.description: Ground floor. A long oak table with seven chairs, a speaker's podium, and benches along the walls for public observers. The table has been scarred by every contentious vote in Millhaven's history.

layout.name: The Records Office
layout.description: Upper floor. Lestelle's domain. Filing cabinets organized by color, date, and category. Every tax record, land deed, birth certificate, and complaint since Millhaven's founding. She knows where everything is.

layout.name: The Advocate's Bench
layout.description: A desk in the corner of the council chamber where Sylvain the Advocate sits during disputes. Not an official position — he simply started showing up and no one could make him stop.

layout.name: The Notice Board
layout.description: Outside the front door. Job postings, lost items, merchant advertisements, council decrees, and the occasional anonymous grievance. Lestelle curates it.

hooks: A land dispute between two farmers has escalated to the point where Sylvain is involved — never a good sign
Lestelle has discovered a discrepancy in the tax records from three years ago that implicates a council member
Aldric Brightblade's merchant guild petitions keep getting more elaborate and less coherent
The council is debating whether to fund a second bridge over the Stoneback — the vote is deadlocked
Someone has been stealing notices from the board and replacing them with obscene poetry

worldContext.location: Millhaven Town Hall, Market Square (north side), Millhaven
